Page.Settings.Title: Settings
About.Title: About
About.Subtitle: OrientPyx is a desktop application for running orienteering competitions: participants, groups, courses, chips, finish read-out, results calculation and protocol printing.
About.Author: Author
About.Tribute.Title: Acknowledgement
About.Tribute.BodyBefore: This program came to be thanks to
About.Tribute.BodyAfter: , who once wrote the «Orientir» program. Our deep gratitude and everlasting memory to him for it. Much of OrientPyx was rethought precisely from that program — from how things were done and implemented there.

Page.StartProtocolJudges.Title: Judges' start protocol

Page.Splits.Title: Splits (HTML)
Page.Splits.Text: Export the day's split protocol to an HTML file.
Splits.Info: Set-course classes get a split-times table by control. Free-order (rogaine) classes get each competitor's controls in punch order. The file is saved as HTML (UTF-8) and opens in any browser.
Splits.Header.Title: Protocol header
Splits.Header.Hint: Blank fields are filled from the competition data and the selected day.
Splits.Header.Subtitle: Subtitle (organisation / club)
Splits.Col.Name: Name
Page.Draw.Title: Start draw
Page.Draw.Text: Prepare the start draw and assign start minutes to the day's competitors.
Draw.StartGroups.Hint: Cluster groups into start lanes. Groups sharing a first control point must stay in one lane. Drag groups between lanes with the mouse, or use “Auto” for a balanced split or the arrows to move them.
Draw.ProportionalHeights: Group height proportional to competitor count
Draw.RemoveStartGroup: Remove start group (its groups move to the first lane)
Draw.Run.ConfirmMessage: The draw will replace the start time of every participant on this day. Continue?
Draw.Col.Name: Name
Draw.Clash.Tooltip: Why is this highlighted? Show the overlapping groups
Draw.Clash.Title: Why «{0}» is highlighted
Draw.Clash.Intro.FirstControl: Group «{0}» opens on control {1} on the same minute as another group in a different start lane. Two competitors would punch that control at the same time.
Draw.Clash.Intro.Course: Group «{0}» runs the same course (identical control order) as another group starting on the same minutes in a different lane. The competitors would run the same course side by side.
Draw.Clash.Peer.Heading: «{0}» — lane {1}
Draw.Clash.HowBody: Move one of the groups to a different start lane so their start minutes no longer overlap, or change the global start / interval or the course control order. The highlight clears once the overlap is gone.
Page.ClassicDraw.Title: Classic draw
Page.ClassicDraw.Text: Per-group draw: pick the groups, set each group's start time and interval, then run the draw for the selected groups only.
ClassicDraw.Run.ConfirmMessage: The draw will replace the start time of every participant in the selected groups. Continue?
ClassicDraw.Error.NoGroups: Select at least one group to draw.
Help.EntryFees.Groups.Title: Per-group entry fees
Help.EntryFees.Groups.What: The base start-entry fee for each group (age/course category) — a separate amount per competition day. Groups themselves are created and edited on the «Groups» page; here you only set their price. The fee is the same for every day: if a runner competes in the group on two days, the base fee is counted twice.
Help.EntryFees.Groups.Why: This is the starting point of the whole calculation. This amount is multiplied by the number of days, discounts and (if any) the raised fee are applied to it, and chip rental is added. A group with no fee set counts as 0.
Help.EntryFees.Groups.How:
The table lists every group in the competition (read-only «Group» column) and a «Entry fee» field.
Enter the fee for each group; an empty field = 0.
The value is saved automatically.
For each day a participant is entered in this group, one such fee is added to their total.
To add or remove a group itself, go to the «Groups» page.
Help.EntryFees.Raised.Title: Raised entry fee
Help.EntryFees.Raised.What: A surcharge for a late entry (registration after the pre-entry deadline). It is a fixed amount added to a participant's fee ONCE (not per day, and not a percentage). It is turned on with a separate checkbox — while it is off the amount field is disabled and the surcharge applies to no one.
Help.EntryFees.Raised.Why: It lets you account for late registrations separately and charge them more without changing the groups' base fees. The «late» flag is set manually for each participant.
Help.EntryFees.Raised.How:
Tick «Apply a raised (late) entry fee» and enter the surcharge amount.
An extra «Raised fee» column then appears in the participants table, with a checkbox next to each participant.
Tick it for those who registered late: the entered amount is added once to their total.
If you turn the block's checkbox off, the column disappears and the surcharge is no longer counted.
Discounts do NOT affect this surcharge — it is added after discounts are applied.
Help.EntryFees.Chips.Title: Chip rental
Help.EntryFees.Chips.What: The rental price of an electronic chip (SI card) per day for participants who have no chip of their own and take a rental one. The «Base price per day» applies to all rental chips by default. Below you can add exceptions: «note → price per day» rows that override the price for chips with a given note (e.g. a pricier chip model, or a free judge's chip).
Help.EntryFees.Chips.Why: Chip rental is a separate part of the total, alongside the group fee. It is added only for the days a participant is issued a rental chip (an own chip = 0 rental), so the rental amount is counted automatically, with no manual tallying.
Help.EntryFees.Chips.How:
Enter the «Base price per day» — it applies to every rental chip for every day it is used.
To set a different price for particular chips, click «Add price» and fill the row: «Note» (the same as on the rental chip's card on the «Chips» page) and «Price per day». Chips with that note are then counted at this price instead of the base one.
Rental is added to the participants-table total only for days where the participant has a rental (not own) chip.
A discount reduces the rental only if that discount has «Applies to chip rental» turned on.
Help.EntryFees.Discounts.Title: Discounts
Help.EntryFees.Discounts.What: Percentage discounts on the start-entry fee — e.g. «FSOU member discount», «IDP discount», «Armed Forces discount». Each discount has a name, a percentage (0–100) and an «Applies to chip rental» flag (whether it also reduces the chip rental, not just the group fee). The «FSOU member» discount is permanent: it cannot be deleted and applies automatically to every participant marked «FSOU member».
Help.EntryFees.Discounts.Why: Discounts reduce the amount due per the organiser's rules. If a participant has several discounts ticked, they are NOT summed — only the single largest by percentage applies. 100% means a free entry.
Help.EntryFees.Discounts.How:
Click «Add discount» and fill in «Name», «Percent» and, if needed, turn on «Applies to chip rental».
Each discount you create (except the automatic FSOU one) adds a separate checkbox column with its name to the participants table.
Tick it for the participants the discount applies to; the total uses the largest of the ticked discounts.
The FSOU discount has no column of its own — it turns on automatically via the «FSOU member» checkbox on the participant's card.
By default a discount reduces only the entry fee; it touches chip rental only when «Applies to chip rental» is on.
Help.Settings.Appearance.Title: Interface appearance
Help.Settings.Appearance.What: The interface scale (font and element size) and the application language (Ukrainian / English).
Help.Settings.Appearance.Why: Lets you fit the app to your screen and preferred language. Changes apply immediately and affect every competition.
Help.Settings.Appearance.How:
Drag the slider to enlarge or shrink the interface; the percentage next to it shows the current scale.
Click a language button to switch the interface — all text is translated at once.
Settings are saved automatically and remembered between launches.
Help.Settings.Paths.Title: Data paths
Help.Settings.Paths.What: The folder where competitions are stored (./events). Each competition is its own subfolder with its own database (event.db), imported files and saved table views.
Help.Settings.Paths.Why: Defines where all competition data physically lives. Change it to keep data on another drive, in a shared folder, or to move the archive between computers.
Help.Settings.Paths.How:
Enter the path to the competitions folder (by default, next to the application).
Click «Save» — a green confirmation mark appears next to it.
The competition list is built by scanning this folder, so after changing the path it refreshes to the competitions found there.
Make sure the folder exists and is writable.
Help.Settings.Online.Title: Online service connection
Help.Settings.Online.What: Access keys for the online service (Supabase) that results are published to for the spectator site: the project URL, the secret service-role key, the public spectator-site URL and the refresh interval. This is an app-level setting shared across all competitions.
Help.Settings.Online.Why: Without these keys the «Online results» feature cannot publish (its «Start» button stays disabled). The secret key grants write access to the database, so it is stored only locally on this computer and is never sent anywhere else.
Help.Settings.Online.How:
Paste the Supabase project URL (e.g. https://xxxxx.supabase.co).
Paste the service-role key — it is masked with dots and stays only on this PC.
Enter the public spectator-site URL (used to build links) and the refresh interval in seconds (−/+ buttons).
Click «Save».
Then go to «Online results» to configure the specific competition and start publishing.
Help.Settings.Readout.Title: Readout type
Help.Settings.Readout.What: The timing system whose readout files the app reads: SportIdent (SPORTident Reader export in «Config+ (card readout)» format, UTF-8) or Sport Time (windows-1251, weekday in parentheses after the time). An application-level setting shared across competitions.
Help.Settings.Readout.Why: Different timing systems export CSV in different formats and encodings. The selected type decides which parser the «Chips» and «Finish read-out» pages use.
Help.Settings.Readout.How:
Pick the system you use at the event.
The change applies immediately — no restart.
If the read-out shows no rows, check that the right system is selected here and the export format in the timing software itself.
Help.OnlineResults.Options.Title: Publish options
Help.OnlineResults.Options.What: The settings of this particular competition for the online table: the short page address (slug), title and subtitle, turning on the summary standings and the «Points» column, and the set of columns of the spectator table — separately for the large screen (venue board) and the phone. These are stored in the competition's database and are independent of the connection keys.
Help.OnlineResults.Options.Why: Determines exactly what spectators see and how. The slug forms the page link; the title/subtitle become the header; the column set controls which data is sent to the site (display_config).
Help.OnlineResults.Options.How:
Set a slug (Latin letters, e.g. kyiv2025) — the public link is built from it.
Enter the page title and subtitle.
In the preview, drag column headers to reorder them; below, turn each column on separately for the large screen (Lg) and the phone (Sm).
If the «Points» column is on, make sure a points rule is configured for the day/groups — otherwise the column will be empty.
Click «Save» — the options are stored for this competition.
Help.OnlineResults.Publish.Title: Start publishing
Help.OnlineResults.Publish.What: Control of the background publishing process: «Start»/«Stop» buttons and links to the spectator page. Once started, the app periodically (at the interval set in «Settings») builds a snapshot of the active day's results and uploads it online.
Help.OnlineResults.Publish.Why: This is the actual broadcast switch. «Start» is disabled until the connection keys are filled in «Settings». Publishing runs in the background, so you can work on other pages.
Help.OnlineResults.Publish.How:
Click «Start» — periodic uploading begins (a green indicator = running).
Copy a link from the list and share it with spectators or open it in a browser.
Click «Stop» to end updates.
The process state is also shown in the background-activity block on the top bar; the publish log is further down the page.
Help.Ranks.Conditions.Title: Rank award conditions
Help.Ranks.Conditions.What: The conditions under which a result in a group is eligible for a sport rank at all: the minimum number of participants in the group, the minimum number of regions represented, and how many top places «count» for a rank. These are app-level settings shared across all competitions.
Help.Ranks.Conditions.Why: By the rules a rank is awarded only with sufficient turnout and geography. If a group does not meet the conditions, the earned rank is not printed for it in the protocols.
Help.Ranks.Conditions.How:
Set the minimum participants and minimum regions required for ranks to be valid in a group.
Specify how many prize places are taken into account.
Values are saved automatically and applied when computing the earned rank for each group.
Help.Ranks.Qualification.Title: Qualification table
Help.Ranks.Qualification.What: The table that determines which rank is awarded depending on the group level (adult/junior), the number of masters in the group and the place taken. From it the app computes the «earned rank» for each participant.
Help.Ranks.Qualification.Why: This is the main source of rank-award rules. By editing the rows you configure which place, and under which conditions, earns each rank — per the current regulation (Annex 89).
Help.Ranks.Qualification.How:
«Add row» creates a new table entry.
Fill in the conditions (level, number of masters, place) and the rank to award.
The computed rank is shown in the protocols and the participants table.
The table is shared across all competitions.
Help.ProtocolLayout.Title: Page orientation
Help.ProtocolLayout.What: The sheet orientation for the printed protocol (.docx): portrait or landscape. The «Landscape» checkbox switches the page to the wide format.
Help.ProtocolLayout.Why: Landscape fits more columns per row and suits wide protocols; portrait suits narrow ones. The preview below immediately shows the sheet in the chosen orientation, as it will look in print.
Help.ProtocolLayout.How:
Turn on «Landscape» for a wide sheet, or leave it off for portrait.
The sheet in the preview changes accordingly.
The setting is stored separately for each day (via «Save» at the bottom).
Help.ProtocolPreview.Title: Document preview
Help.ProtocolPreview.What: A live mock-up of the protocol in true sheet proportions, filled with the selected day's real data. Here you can edit the header text in place (organisation, name, title, date/type/venue), reorder columns by dragging their headers, and show/hide columns with the checkboxes under the mock-up.
Help.ProtocolPreview.Why: This is the template configuration: what you see in the mock-up is what goes into the generated .docx. No need to export blindly — edits are visible at once.
Help.ProtocolPreview.How:
Click the header text to edit it (Enter inserts a new line).
Drag a column header to change the order.
Untick a checkbox under the mock-up to hide a column.
Click «Save» to fix the template for this day; the generate button at the top produces the .docx.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Settings.Title: Standings settings
Help.SummaryProtocol.Settings.What: The rules of the multi-day summary standings: the mode (by total points or by total time), the requirement to have started on all days, and the priority day for breaking ties.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Settings.Why: Determines how several days are combined into one result and how participants are ranked. «Require all days» excludes those who missed a day; the priority day decides who ranks higher on an equal total.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Settings.How:
Choose the mode: by points (sum of scores) or by time (sum of times).
Turn on «Require all days» if only participants of every day are ranked.
Choose a priority day for the tie-break.
The settings are stored for this competition.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Preview.Title: Summary protocol preview
Help.SummaryProtocol.Preview.What: A live mock-up of the summary protocol with a two-tier header (a band per day over the Place/Time/Points columns), filled with real data. The header text is editable in place and the order of the leading columns is changed by dragging.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Preview.Why: Shows exactly which summary document will be generated, given the chosen standings mode — so you can check the look before exporting to .docx.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Preview.How:
Click the header text to edit it.
Drag a leading column header to change the order.
Check that the day columns and the total match what you expect.
The generate button at the top produces the summary .docx.
Help.Draw.Params.Title: Draw parameters
Help.Draw.Params.What: The general parameters of the start-time draw: the start time of the first runner, the interval between starts (mm:ss) and the separation criterion for teammates — by region, club or team, so that «own» runners do not start back to back.
Help.Draw.Params.Why: These parameters set the start rhythm and prevent runners from the same region/club starting one after another. They apply to the whole day's draw.
Help.Draw.Params.How:
Enter the mass-start time (hh:mm:ss) and the interval between start minutes.
Choose the separation criterion (or «no separation»).
Once set, run the draw in the start-groups block below.
Help.Draw.StartGroups.Title: Start groups (lanes)
Help.Draw.StartGroups.What: The competition's groups combined into start lanes. Here you set the number of lanes (with the stepper) and run the draw itself — a random assignment of a start minute to each participant, respecting the configured separation.
Help.Draw.StartGroups.Why: Lanes let several groups start in parallel with separated minutes, to use the start time efficiently. This is where the draw result is written to the participants.
Help.Draw.StartGroups.How:
Set the number of lanes with the stepper (or use automatic grouping).
Run the draw — each participant is assigned a start minute.
The start time is written to the participant's day and appears in the start protocols.
If needed, re-run the draw to redraw the times.

Page.Monitor.Title: Results monitor
Page.Monitor.Text: Generate HTML result screens for venue monitors: each file shows the groups you choose, with auto-scroll and auto-refresh. Files are saved in the folder of the day whose results they show — open them with the “Open in browser” button.
Monitor.NoDay: No competition day selected — generation uses the active day.
Monitor.Files.Title: Files
Monitor.File.Name: File name (.html)
Monitor.File.Title: Page title
Monitor.File.GroupsHint: Tick the groups for this file. None or all ticked means every group of the day.
Monitor.File.ColumnsHint: Shared by all files. Tick columns to show; drag a header in the preview to reorder.
Monitor.Preview.Empty: No results to show — pick groups that have participants on this day.
Monitor.Log.NoFiles: No enabled file with a path — add a file.
Activity.Monitor.Title: Results monitor

FinishRead.AutoRead.Error.WrongFormat: The file is not in the expected format for the selected readout type. Check the readout type in Settings or the export format in the timing software.

FinishRead.Problematic.Hint: Tick the controls that stopped working. They become optional: a runner who missed one is not penalised, and the splits show them as disabled.

CoursePattern.Help.Title: Control order (mixed discipline)
CoursePattern.Help.Intro: For the mixed discipline the «Control order» field is a pattern that combines a prescribed order with free-choice sections. It is checked like a set course, but by the rules below. Foreign and extra punches between the required controls are ignored.
CoursePattern.Help.SeqBody: List the controls separated by spaces — they must be punched in exactly this order. Angle brackets < > mark an ordered section inside a larger pattern.
CoursePattern.Help.AnyTitle: Any N of a list (free choice)
CoursePattern.Help.AnyBody: [N: a b c …] — any N of the listed controls, in any order. The number N before the colon is how many of them must be punched.
CoursePattern.Help.MarkersBody: You may write the start at the beginning and the finish at the end (S … F). It is optional: start and finish are defined by control type, and the preview always shows the order as «S … F».
CoursePattern.Help.NestBody: Blocks can be nested inside one another, combining prescribed order and free choice.
CoursePattern.Help.NotesBody:
• Start and finish in the pattern are optional — they are defined by control type.
• «Problematic» controls (disabled for the day) are treated as visited automatically and never penalise.
• The control count shows how many punches the pattern requires (for [N: …] it is N).
• The number of spaces does not matter; brackets must be balanced.
CoursePattern.Error.ChoiceMissingColon: A choice block needs a colon after the count, e.g. [2: 31 32 33]: {0}
CoursePattern.Error.ChoiceBadCount: Invalid count in a choice block [N: …]: «{0}»
CoursePattern.Error.EmptyChoiceBlock: Empty choice block [N: …] — no controls listed
CoursePattern.Error.ChoiceCountTooLarge: A choice block requires more controls than are listed: {0}
Sheet.Sort.Empty: No sorting set. Add a level to sort first by one column, then by another.
Settings.Online.Title: Online results (Supabase)
Settings.Online.Hint: Keys for publishing live results. Stored only locally. Shared across all competitions.
Settings.Online.ServiceKeyHint: Grants write access. Never share it — the spectator site uses only the public anon key.
Settings.Readout.Title: Readout type
Settings.Readout.Hint: The timing system whose files the read-out is taken from. Determines the readout file format. Shared across competitions.
Settings.Update.Title: Version & updates
Settings.Update.DevHint: Updates are only available in the installed build of the app.
Page.Settings.Update.UpToDate: You are on the latest version.
Page.Settings.Update.Failed: Update failed. Please try again later.

CreateEvent.Error.Invalid: Identifier must be a valid folder name.

ImportEvent.NameTaken: That name is already taken or invalid.
ImportEvent.BadArchive: The selected file is not an OrientPyx competition archive.
Help.Tooltip: Help: what this screen is and how to use it
Help.Dashboard.Title: Dashboard
Help.Dashboard.What: The competition's home screen: a summary of the active day's statistics (participants, groups, chips, read-outs, finishes) and quick buttons to jump to the main sections.
Help.Dashboard.Why: Gives an at-a-glance picture of the competition's state and speeds up jumping to the most frequent actions while the start is running.
Help.Dashboard.How:
Pick a day in the drop-down on the right if there are several days.
Click a stat tile to jump to the matching section (e.g. «without chip» opens a filtered participant list).
The quick-action buttons at the top lead to the draw, start protocol, read-out and so on.
«Refresh» re-reads the stats if the data changed in another section.
Help.CompetitionInfo.Title: Competition information
Help.CompetitionInfo.What: The competition card: name, venue, dates, plus the officials (chief judge, secretary, jury) and the default course setter.
Help.CompetitionInfo.Why: These details are inserted into the headers and signatures of every protocol and into the online results publication.
Help.CompetitionInfo.How:
Fill in the fields and click «Save».
The officials and course setter are printed in the protocols' signature block.
The course setter can be overridden for a particular group on the «Groups» screen.
Help.CompetitionDays.Title: Competition days
Help.CompetitionDays.What: The list of the competition's days (stages). Each day has its own number, date, discipline and its own data folder.
Help.CompetitionDays.Why: Many sections (groups, read-out, protocols) work in the context of the selected day; here you define the days themselves and their disciplines.
Help.CompetitionDays.How:
«Add» — a new competition day.
Choose the day's discipline — it determines how results are computed.
«Change number» reorders the day and renames its data folder.
Deleting a day removes the day and its links; be careful.
Help.ControlPoints.Title: Control points
Help.ControlPoints.What: The list of the course's control points (CP): code, type (start/finish/regular) and, if needed, coordinates.
Help.ControlPoints.Why: CPs make up the groups' courses; their codes are checked against the chip read-outs, and the coordinates give the course length.
Help.ControlPoints.How:
«Add» — a new CP; enter the code and type.
«Import» — load CPs from an IOF XML file.
Turn on «Map coordinates» to edit the coordinates.
Double-click a cell to edit its value.
Help.Groups.Title: Groups
Help.Groups.What: The competition's groups (classes): name, course (CP order), age limits and the group's course setter.
Help.Groups.Why: Participants are split into groups; the course and discipline determine the punch check and how results are computed within the group.
Help.Groups.How:
«Add» — a new group; day membership is set separately.
Enter the course (CP code order) in the matching column.
For the mixed discipline, the «?» button in the course editor explains the order format.
Age limits (birth year from/to) highlight participants who don't fit.
Help.Chips.Title: Chips
Help.Chips.What: The register of rental SportIdent chips: numbers, notes and automatic reading of chips from a file.
Help.Chips.Why: Rental chips are issued to participants without their own chip; auto-reading picks up new punches from the file during the competition.
Help.Chips.How:
«Add» / «Add range» — enter rental-chip numbers.
Configure auto-reading (file and interval) and start it.
Double-click a number to reassign the chip (with confirmation).
«Clear» removes every chip from the list.
Help.FinishRead.Title: Finish read-out
Help.FinishRead.What: The log of chip read-outs at the finish: start/finish time, CP punches, status and, for the selected row, the course passage layout (splits).
Help.FinishRead.Why: This is the primary source of results: read-outs determine time, punch correctness and a participant's place.
Help.FinishRead.How:
Read-outs are added automatically from the file; pick the day at the top.
Select a row to see the passage splits below.
«Problem CPs» — disable broken CPs so they don't count as a miss.
Double-click / row action to edit the read-out (chip, time, status) or print a split.
Help.Participants.Title: Participants
Help.Participants.What: The competition's participant register: two views — the selected day's table and the «Roster» (a consolidated printout across all days). Shows computed results (start, finish, status, place, score).
Help.Participants.Why: This is the central list of people: here you add, import and edit participants and assign them numbers and chips.
Help.Participants.How:
«Import» — load participants from UOF/IOF XML or CSV/Excel.
«Actions» — bulk operations: assigning numbers, handing out chips, bulk-editing a column.
«Add» — a new participant; double-click to edit a cell.
«Export» saves the current view to CSV/Excel.
Help.Regions.Title: Regions
Help.Regions.What: The directory of regions (oblasts) participants are attached to.
Help.Regions.Why: The region is used in the protocols and for separating participants during the draw.
Help.Regions.How:
«Add» — a new region (name must be unique).
A region is assigned to participants in their table via a drop-down.
Deleting a region clears it from every participant.
Help.Clubs.Title: Clubs
Help.Clubs.What: The directory of clubs (teams) participants belong to.
Help.Clubs.Why: The club is printed in the protocols and taken into account when separating participants during the draw.
Help.Clubs.How:
«Add» — a new club (name must be unique).
A club is assigned to a participant in their table via a drop-down.
Deleting a club clears it from every participant.
Help.Dussh.Title: Sports schools
Help.Dussh.What: The directory of sports schools (ДЮСШ) participants are attached to.
Help.Dussh.Why: The sports school is used in reporting and the protocols.
Help.Dussh.How:
«Add» — a new sports school (name must be unique).
Assigned to a participant via a drop-down in their table.
Deleting it clears the value from every participant.
Help.Ranks.Title: Ranks
Help.Ranks.What: The directory of sports ranks (shared across all competitions).
Help.Ranks.Why: A participant's rank is picked from the list; the directory defines the possible values.
Help.Ranks.How:
«Add» — a new rank.
A rank is assigned to a participant in their table via a drop-down.
This directory is shared across all competitions (stored at the application level).
Help.Points.Title: Points
Help.Points.What: Rules for awarding ranking points: by a placement table or a custom formula (with variables for time, participant count and place).
Help.Points.Why: Lets you award points for a result, for rankings and summary standings.
Help.Points.How:
On the left is the list of rules; select a rule to edit it on the right.
Choose the mode: placement table or formula.
Fill in the values (points per place) or enter a formula.
The app ships with a set of ready-made tables — they can be edited.
Help.EntryFees.Title: Entry fees
Help.EntryFees.What: Entry-fee calculation: per-group base fees, discounts (e.g. for FSOU members) and surcharges, plus the chip rental price.
Help.EntryFees.Why: Automatically computes the total amount due for each participant in the participants table.
Help.EntryFees.How:
Set the fee for each group in the first table.
Configure discounts and surcharges; the FSOU discount applies automatically.
The chip rental price is set separately.
The total and its components are shown in the participants table in separate columns.
Help.Protocols.Title: Result protocols
Help.Protocols.What: Building the per-group results protocol with export to Word (.docx) and a live document preview.
Help.Protocols.Why: This is the day's official result: ordered places, times, scores and judges' signatures in printable form.
Help.Protocols.How:
Pick the day at the top.
In the preview, drag column headers to change their order; turn off the unneeded ones.
The template (orientation, columns, header) is stored separately for each day.
The generate button creates the .docx protocol file.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Title: Multi-day summary protocol
Help.SummaryProtocol.What: The overall standings across several days: a consolidated table with a two-tier header (days above the Place/Time/Points columns) and an overall place.
Help.SummaryProtocol.Why: Determines the overall result of a multi-day competition by total score or total time.
Help.SummaryProtocol.How:
Choose the standings mode: by points or by time.
«Require all days» excludes those who did not start on every day.
Set a priority day to break ties.
The generate button creates the consolidated .docx protocol.
Help.StartProtocols.Title: Start protocols
Help.StartProtocols.What: The start protocol in Word (.docx) with a live preview. Two kinds: regular (by group, ordered by start time) and judges' (by start minute).
Help.StartProtocols.Why: Provides an ordered list of starts for announcing and controlling at the start.
Help.StartProtocols.How:
Pick the day at the top; the start time is taken from the draw.
In the preview, drag column headers and turn off the unneeded ones.
The template is stored separately for each day and protocol kind.
The generate button creates the .docx file.
Help.OnlineResults.Title: Online results
Help.OnlineResults.What: Background publishing of the active day's computed results online (Supabase), from where a separate spectator site reads them.
Help.OnlineResults.Why: Lets spectators see results in real time without waiting for printed protocols.
Help.OnlineResults.How:
The connection keys (URL, key, public site URL) are set on the «Settings» screen.
Configure the publish options (title, subtitle, column set) for this competition.
Start publishing — it runs in the background (activity icon at the top).
Status and stop are visible in the background-processes block on the top bar.
Help.Monitor.Title: Results on monitor
Help.Monitor.What: Generation of self-contained HTML result screens with auto-scroll and auto-refresh for display on a monitor/projector.
Help.Monitor.Why: Shows results on a large screen in the hall without manual refreshing.
Help.Monitor.How:
Pick the day at the top.
Configure which groups go into which file, and the column set.
Generate the HTML files and open them in a browser on the monitor.
The files refresh and scroll the results by themselves.
Help.Splits.Title: Splits (HTML)
Help.Splits.What: Export of the day's course-passage layout (splits) to modern HTML: a CP table with cumulative time and places, or a per-participant layout for rogaine.
Help.Splits.Why: Gives a detailed leg-by-leg analysis of the passage — where time was lost and who led at each CP.
Help.Splits.How:
Pick the day at the top.
The export button saves the splits HTML file.
In the table, the leader and the best leg time are highlighted; a tooltip shows the time loss.
The look depends on the day's discipline (set CP order or rogaine).
Help.Draw.Title: Start draw
Help.Draw.What: Start-time draw: groups are combined into start lanes, and each participant is assigned a start minute with teammate separation.
Help.Draw.Why: Forms the participants' start times — the basis of the start protocols and judging at the start.
Help.Draw.How:
Pick the day at the top.
Set the parameters (interval, lanes) and the separation criterion (region/club/team).
Run the draw — a start time is written to each participant.
The result is then used in the start protocols.
Help.ClassicDraw.Title: Classic draw
Help.ClassicDraw.What: A manually controlled per-group draw: you pick the groups and separation parameters and assign start minutes.
Help.ClassicDraw.Why: Gives more control over the draw than automatic grouping into lanes.
Help.ClassicDraw.How:
Pick the day at the top.
Choose the separation criterion and tick the needed groups («Select all/none»).
Run the draw for the selected groups.
The start time is written to the participants and carried into the start protocols.
Help.Settings.Title: Settings
Help.Settings.What: General application settings: interface size, language, data paths, split printing and the online-service connection keys.
Help.Settings.Why: Here you set the parameters shared across all competitions and independent of the selected day.
Help.Settings.How:
Change the interface size with the slider and the language — they apply immediately.
Set the folders for data (./data) and competitions (./events) as needed.
Enter the online-service keys (URL, key) for publishing results.
«Close» returns to the working screen.
